headlines.title: Japan, U.S. Reaffirm Currency Cooperation Amid Weakening Yen
Japanese yen starting to slip away again, will Tokyo officials step in?
investingLive Asia-Pacific FX news wrap: Oil and the USD held firm, US-Iran pessimism
Japanese Yen softens ahead of US CPI inflation data
Japan's March household spending falls 2.9% on year, down for 4th month
Bank of Japan debated rate hike at April meeting, June raise in focus
Global Market: Bank of Japan turns increasingly hawkish as oil shock fuels inflation concerns

marketTone.ko: 일본 증시는 엔화 약세가 지속되며 미국과의 통화 협력 재확인 및 당국 개입 가능성에 대한 관측이 커지고 있습니다. 일본은행은 4월 회의에서 금리 인상을 논의했으며, 유가 충격발 인플레이션 우려로 6월 인상 가능성에 무게가 실리는 등 매파적 기조를 강화하고 있습니다. 다만, 3월 가계 지출이 4개월 연속 감소하는 등 내수 부진은 이어지고 있으며, 미국 CPI 발표와 달러 강세 또한 엔화에 영향을 미칠 주요 변수로 작용하고 있습니다.
marketTone.en: The Japanese market is grappling with a persistently weak yen, prompting reaffirmation of currency cooperation with the U.S. and speculation about potential intervention. This comes as the Bank of Japan shows an increasingly hawkish stance, having debated a rate hike at its April meeting with a June raise now in focus, driven by oil shock-fueled inflation concerns. However, domestic economic data remains soft, with March household spending falling for the fourth consecutive month, while upcoming US CPI data and broader USD strength also weigh on the yen.
